information: FICSIT Foreman v0.17.0 — DEMAND-PULL ROUTING CORE REWRITE (shipped, tagged, CI green, 107 PASS, LSP clean). User verdict after the v0.16.3–0.16.6 whack-a-mole arc: "why do you insist on making loops that block everything, when we have events... only mergers and splitter incomes and constructors/assemblers craftings+inputs". Full transport rewrite per spec devbox/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-foreman-demand-pull-routing.md. ARCHITECTURE: (1) DEMAND re-derived per epoch from ground truth — machine demands ingredient when live input < lowCrafts(2) crafts, refill to hiCrafts(6), capped by remaining product share; buffer demands staged slice capped by PHYSICAL CAPACITY (no overproduction, ever — reroute/sink churn eliminated at the source). (2) nextHop = per-item multi-source BFS from demanders (top-3 legs, per-dispatch rotation within equal-distance groups for exact fair splits). (3) ItemRequest event → head item → nextHop → transferItem; full leg → divert to next-nearest or hold (backpressure). (4) applyGates: providers (sources + stocked hubs + CONTENT-BASED unclassified relay containers) granted shortfall batches, FIN metering = stay blocked + unblockedTransfers budget (UNBLOCKING = unbounded release, the first big bug). (5) Deleted: orders/term/ratio, flowWindow/_contribution, auth/deliv ledgers, gateSources stall clamp+amnesty, buildQuota/_orderRoom, overflow RECOVER, balanced-room delivery, _truncateOrders. LITE shrank 87KB→78.7KB. KEPT: assignment dwell/yield-helpful/drain lifecycle, port pins (Kuhn), live-recipe leg gate, hold patience, jam-mark aging, namer/discover. KEY BUILD LESSONS: (a) planner reuse: machine claims (busy) MUST reset per epoch — accumulating claims silently killed deep-craft on epoch 2+ while epoch 1 worked (probe at the right epoch!); (b) demand placement must be ATOMIC (stage entries+sub-claims+setRecipes in a txn, commit only when the whole recursive chain is suppliable) — the old order-rollback semantics, re-learned; (c) supply checks need REACHABILITY (firstHopTo cached) — a stocked source with no belt path is no supply, else recipe restores churn forever; (d) deep-craft stages sized to exactly what the parent pulls (needUnits threading) or hi-water slack compounds per stage; (e) transfer-credits are NOT deliveries — freeze detection must use input-SIGNATURE stability (count+composition), not delivery counters; (f) legacy single-shot tests → epoch loops (feedback converges over epochs: exact final state, bounded release = true demand + one hi-water band); legacy ledger-exactness asserts are mechanism locks, not behavior. TEST MIGRATION: retired deduct/gatemulti/pdbudget/settle/stallbp/phantom/portcap with the code they locked; ingbalance rewritten as per-ingredient low-water no-hoard lock; reroute rewritten as capacity-capped-demand lock. NEXT: live smoke on the user's factory (re-paste LITE 0.17.0); the structural priority question (bulk terminal jobs vs smart-plating chain) may resurface but demand-pull changes its shape (no more rank-driven release floods).
